title: Install Replacement Motor Onto Gearbox
summary: Install a replacement motor onto the gearbox using the documented shaft alignment, clamp collar positioning, mounting fasteners, orientation, torque values, electrical reconnection, and restart preparation steps from the motor installation section.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a replacement motor is being installed onto the gearbox as part of the documented motor replacement process for the OptiSweep A-axis or Z-axis.
not_for: Do not use when the correct motor variant for A-axis left, A-axis right, Z-axis left, or Z-axis right cannot be verified.
Do not use as a substitute for the separate motor configuration procedure when manufacturer application configuration is required.
Do not use as a substitute for the separate operator station startup procedure; this source only references that procedure.
Do not use if the motor cannot be fully inserted with the documented shaft and clamp collar or bushing alignment.

responsibilities: Perform mechanical motor installation onto the gearbox.
Verify the replacement motor variant matches the target axis and side.
Align the shaft, bushing, and clamp collar as documented.
Apply specified fasteners and torque values.
Determine whether separate motor configuration steps are required and follow the referenced procedure if needed.
Reinstall guarding if necessary and restart the operator station using the documented startup procedure.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the candidate.
Do not over-tighten the clamp collar during the temporary tightening step.
Re-install the top and or bottom side guarding, if necessary, before returning the system toward service.
The source section references separate startup and configuration procedures that are not included here.
access_or_tools_needed: 4-mm hex wrench for Z-axis clamp collar
5-mm hex wrench for A-axis clamp collar
Torque tool capable of 7 Nm, 9.5 Nm, and 16.5 Nm
Manufacturer application if motor configuration is required
Access to operator station startup procedure

access_required: Access to the motor electrical connection points
Access to the operator station startup procedure
Access to the manufacturer application if motor configuration is required
system_preconditions: Replacement motor is available for installation.
Correct motor variant can be identified for A-axis left, A-axis right, Z-axis left, or Z-axis right.
Motor installation point on the gearbox is accessible.
Top and or bottom side guarding can be reinstalled if necessary after installation.
external_references: Documented configuration steps for the motor if the motor has not been configured with the manufacturer application.
Documented startup procedure for re-starting the operator station.

visual_references.description: Motor installation diagram showing motor mounting and clamp collar connection points on page 125.
Figure identifying the Teknic SD motor referenced in the motor replacement section.

steps.title: Clean the motor shaft
steps.purpose: Prepare the motor shaft for proper insertion and fit into the gearbox clamp collar or bushing.
steps.instruction: Clean the motor shaft of any grease or debris before installation.
steps.expected_result: The motor shaft is clean and ready for alignment and insertion.
steps.healthy_condition: No grease or debris is present on the motor shaft.
steps.failure_condition: Grease, debris, or contamination remains on the motor shaft.

actions.label: Inspect shaft condition
actions.exact_action: Clean the motor shaft of any grease or debris.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the shaft cannot be cleaned to a condition suitable for installation.

steps.title: Verify the replacement motor variant
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct replacement motor is being installed for the target axis and side.
steps.instruction: Verify the correct motor is being installed: A-axis left, A-axis right, Z-axis left, or Z-axis right.
steps.expected_result: The replacement motor variant matches the intended installation location.
steps.healthy_condition: Motor identity is confirmed for the correct axis and side.
steps.failure_condition: Motor identity cannot be verified or does not match the intended axis and side.

actions.label: Verify motor type
actions.exact_action: Verify the correct motor is being installed: A-axis left, A-axis right, Z-axis left, or Z-axis right.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor identification figure associated with the motor replacement section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the correct motor variant cannot be verified for A-axis left, A-axis right, Z-axis left, or Z-axis right.

source_evidence.short_description: Figure 7-5 identifies the Teknic SD Motor referenced in the motor replacement procedure and software parameter download steps.

steps.title: Align the shaft bushing and clamp collar openings
steps.purpose: Position the gearbox components so the clamp collar fastener can be controlled and the motor shaft can be inserted correctly.
steps.instruction: Inside the gearbox, align the opening in the shaft bushing with the opening in the clamp collar.
steps.expected_result: The shaft bushing opening and clamp collar opening are aligned.
steps.healthy_condition: The openings are visibly aligned and ready for access and shaft insertion.
steps.failure_condition: The openings cannot be aligned or do not remain aligned.

actions.label: Align bushing and collar
actions.exact_action: Inside the gearbox, align the opening in the shaft bushing with the opening in the clamp collar.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Clamp collar and motor mounting area used during motor installation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the shaft bushing opening and clamp collar opening cannot be aligned.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

source_evidence.short_description: Maintenance diagram associated with motor installation during the motor replacement procedure, showing the motor mounting and clamp collar connection points.

steps.title: Insert the hex key through the access hole
steps.purpose: Prevent the clamp collar from rotating while positioning the motor and clamp collar fastener.
steps.instruction: Insert the hex key through the access hole into the clamp collar fastener to help prevent the clamp collar from rotating.
steps.expected_result: The hex key is engaged through the access hole into the clamp collar fastener.
steps.healthy_condition: The clamp collar fastener is accessible and the clamp collar is restrained from rotating.
steps.failure_condition: The access hole cannot be used to engage the clamp collar fastener or the clamp collar still rotates freely.

actions.label: Restrain clamp collar
actions.exact_action: Insert the hex key through the access hole into the clamp collar fastener to help prevent the clamp collar from rotating.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Access hole and clamp collar location on the gearbox during motor installation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the clamp collar fastener cannot be accessed through the access hole.
Stop or escalate if the clamp collar cannot be prevented from rotating.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

Page shows labels for clamp collar, hole access, communication cable, and power cable, and text instructs replacing the access hole plug, reconnecting electrical connections, configuring the motor if needed, reinstalling guarding, and restarting the operator station.

source_evidence.short_description: Maintenance diagram associated with motor installation during the motor replacement procedure, showing the motor mounting and clamp collar connection points.

steps.title: Align and fully insert the motor shaft
steps.purpose: Seat the motor shaft correctly into the clamp collar or bushing using the documented shaft feature alignment.
steps.instruction: Align the key-way or flat in the motor shaft, if present, with the opening in the clamp collar or bushing and fully insert the motor shaft.
steps.expected_result: The motor shaft is fully inserted with the key-way or flat aligned as documented.
steps.healthy_condition: The shaft is fully seated and aligned with the clamp collar or bushing opening.
steps.failure_condition: The shaft cannot be fully inserted or the key-way or flat cannot be aligned.

actions.label: Insert motor shaft
actions.exact_action: Align the key-way or flat in the motor shaft, if present, with the opening in the clamp collar or bushing and fully insert the motor shaft.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor shaft alignment and insertion area at the clamp collar or bushing opening.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the motor cannot be fully inserted with the documented shaft and clamp collar or bushing alignment.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

source_evidence.short_description: Maintenance diagram associated with motor installation during the motor replacement procedure, showing the motor mounting and clamp collar connection points.

steps.title: Temporarily tighten the clamp collar
steps.purpose: Hold the motor shaft in place before final mounting and final clamp collar torque.
steps.instruction: Temporarily tighten the clamp collar and do not over-tighten.
steps.expected_result: The clamp collar is temporarily tightened enough to hold position without over-tightening.
steps.healthy_condition: The clamp collar holds the shaft position and is not over-tightened.
steps.failure_condition: The clamp collar is over-tightened or does not hold the shaft position.

actions.label: Temporary clamp collar tightening
actions.exact_action: Temporarily tighten the clamp collar and do not over-tighten.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Clamp collar connection point used for temporary tightening.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the clamp collar is being over-tightened.
Stop or escalate if temporary tightening does not hold the motor position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

steps.title: Mount the motor to the gearbox
steps.purpose: Secure the motor mechanically to the gearbox using the documented fasteners and torque.
steps.instruction: Mount the motor to the gearbox using four M5 socket-head screws and tighten them to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).
steps.expected_result: The motor is mounted to the gearbox with four M5 socket-head screws tightened to the specified torque.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M5 socket-head screws are installed and tightened to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).
steps.failure_condition: Any mounting screw is missing, loose, damaged, or not tightened to the specified torque.

actions.label: Install mounting screws
actions.exact_action: Mount the motor to the gearbox using four M5 socket-head screws and tighten them to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Four motor-to-gearbox M5 socket-head screw mounting points.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if all four M5 socket-head screws cannot be installed.
Stop or escalate if the specified 7 Nm (62 in-lb) torque cannot be applied.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

source_evidence.short_description: Maintenance diagram associated with motor installation during the motor replacement procedure, showing the motor mounting and clamp collar connection points.

steps.title: Orient the motor with connections facing down
steps.purpose: Set the motor in the documented installation orientation before final clamp collar tightening and reconnection.
steps.instruction: Orient the motor with the electrical connections facing down.
steps.expected_result: The motor is oriented so the electrical connections face down.
steps.healthy_condition: Electrical connections are positioned downward as documented.
steps.failure_condition: Motor orientation does not place the electrical connections facing down.

actions.label: Set motor orientation
actions.exact_action: Orient the motor with the electrical connections facing down.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Correct motor orientation relative to the gearbox and connection end.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the motor cannot be oriented with the electrical connections facing down.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

source_evidence.short_description: Maintenance diagram associated with motor installation during the motor replacement procedure, showing the motor mounting and clamp collar connection points.

steps.title: Apply final clamp collar torque by axis
steps.purpose: Set the clamp collar to the documented final torque for the installed axis.
steps.instruction: Loosen then re-tighten the clamp collar using the axis-specific tool and torque: Z-axis uses a 4-mm hex wrench to 9.5 Nm (84 in-lb); A-axis uses a 5-mm hex wrench to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).
steps.expected_result: The clamp collar is loosened and re-tightened to the correct axis-specific torque using the correct hex wrench.
steps.healthy_condition: Z-axis clamp collar is tightened with a 4-mm hex wrench to 9.5 Nm (84 in-lb), or A-axis clamp collar is tightened with a 5-mm hex wrench to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb), as applicable.
steps.failure_condition: Incorrect tool or torque is used, or the clamp collar cannot be re-tightened to the specified value.

actions.label: Final clamp collar torque
actions.exact_action: Loosen then re-tighten the clamp collar using the axis-specific tool and torque: Z-axis uses a 4-mm hex wrench to 9.5 Nm (84 in-lb); A-axis uses a 5-mm hex wrench to 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Clamp collar location used for final axis-specific tightening.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the correct axis-specific tool is not available.
Stop or escalate if the specified axis-specific torque cannot be applied.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Motor installation steps include shaft and clamp collar alignment, temporary clamp collar tightening, mounting with four M5 socket-head screws to 7 Nm, orienting electrical connections down, and axis-specific clamp collar re-tightening torque.

source_evidence.short_description: Maintenance diagram associated with motor installation during the motor replacement procedure, showing the motor mounting and clamp collar connection points.

steps.title: Replace the access hole plug and reconnect electrical connections
steps.purpose: Restore the gearbox access point and reconnect the motor for operation.
steps.instruction: Replace the access hole plug and reconnect the electrical connections.
steps.expected_result: The access hole plug is replaced and the motor electrical connections are reconnected.
steps.healthy_condition: Access hole plug is installed and both communication and power connections are reconnected as applicable.
steps.failure_condition: Access hole plug is missing or electrical connections are not restored.

actions.label: Restore plug and cables
actions.exact_action: Replace the access hole plug and reconnect the electrical connections.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor installation area associated with access hole and connection end.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the access hole plug cannot be replaced.
Stop or escalate if the electrical connections cannot be reconnected.

steps.title: Configure the motor if required
steps.purpose: Ensure the replacement motor is configured when the source indicates manufacturer application configuration is needed.
steps.instruction: If the motor has not been configured with the manufacturer application, follow the documented configuration steps.
steps.expected_result: The motor is either already configured or the separate documented configuration procedure is followed.
steps.healthy_condition: Motor configuration status is confirmed and any required separate configuration procedure is completed.
steps.failure_condition: Motor configuration is required but the documented configuration steps or application are not available.

actions.label: Check configuration requirement
actions.exact_action: If the motor has not been configured with the manufacturer application, follow the documented configuration steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor figure associated with the Teknic software download and configuration context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if motor configuration with the manufacturer application is required but not available in the provided source section.

steps.title: Reinstall guarding if necessary
steps.purpose: Return protective guarding to place before restart when guarding was removed for access.
steps.instruction: Re-install the top and or bottom side guarding, if necessary.
steps.expected_result: Any removed top and or bottom side guarding is reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: Required guarding is back in place.
steps.failure_condition: Guarding required for the installation area is not reinstalled.

actions.label: Reinstall guarding
actions.exact_action: Re-install the top and or bottom side guarding, if necessary.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if required guarding cannot be reinstalled.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the station toward operation using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the documented startup procedure.
steps.expected_result: The operator station restart is initiated using the separate documented startup procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: Operator station startup procedure is available and followed.
steps.failure_condition: The startup procedure is unavailable or the operator station cannot be restarted using the documented procedure.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the documented startup procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the documented startup procedure is not available.
Stop or escalate if the operator station cannot be restarted using the documented startup procedure.

success_criteria: The replacement motor is installed on the gearbox.
The motor shaft is fully inserted with documented alignment to the clamp collar or bushing.
The motor is mounted using four M5 socket-head screws tightened to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).
The motor is oriented with the electrical connections facing down.
The clamp collar is re-tightened using the correct axis-specific tool and torque.
The access hole plug is replaced and electrical connections are reconnected.
Any required motor configuration is completed through the referenced separate procedure.
The operator station is restarted using the documented startup procedure.
healthy_conditions: Motor variant matches the intended A-axis or Z-axis side.
Shaft, bushing, and clamp collar openings are aligned during installation.
Clamp collar is temporarily tightened without over-tightening.
Final clamp collar torque matches the installed axis: Z-axis 9.5 Nm (84 in-lb) with 4-mm hex wrench, or A-axis 16.5 Nm (146 in-lb) with 5-mm hex wrench.
Electrical connections face down after installation.
Communication and power connections are restored as applicable.
Guarding is reinstalled if it was removed for access.
failure_conditions: Correct motor variant cannot be verified.
Motor shaft cannot be fully inserted with the documented alignment.
Clamp collar is over-tightened during temporary tightening.
Specified mounting or clamp collar torque cannot be applied.
Motor cannot be oriented with electrical connections facing down.
Access hole plug or electrical connections cannot be restored.
Motor configuration is required but the manufacturer application or documented configuration steps are unavailable.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the correct motor variant cannot be verified for A-axis left, A-axis right, Z-axis left, or Z-axis right.
Escalate if the motor cannot be fully inserted with the documented shaft and clamp collar or bushing alignment.
Escalate if motor configuration with the manufacturer application is required but not available in the provided source section.
Escalate if required torque values cannot be applied with the specified tools.
Escalate if the documented startup procedure is not available.
